heading: About me
identity: I'm a Software Engineer who thinks in systems. I don't just complete tasks -- I ask what would really make a difference. When I see fragmented tools, I build a unified platform. When I see a recurring manual process, I automate it structurally.
adhd_content: I have ADHD. That means I bring radical curiosity to every problem, hyperfocus when something genuinely interests me, and the ability to see the whole system while others are looking at a single component. It also means I've learned to organize my thinking deliberately -- which makes me a better engineer, not a worse one.
tech_philosophy: I choose technologies based on the problem, not based on what I already know or feel comfortable with. If the problem calls for Swift, I learn Swift. If it calls for Terraform, I learn Terraform. The tool serves the solution, never the other way around.
philosophy: I read philosophy -- different authors, different schools of thought. It's trained me to reason about complex problems, question assumptions, and think at multiple levels: from the technical implementation to the broader purpose of what I'm building.
values: I value honesty, simplicity, and people who put humanity first. I don't work for money or duty -- I work to build something I'm proud of. I expect mutual respect as a baseline, and I don't compromise on that for any amount of money.
looking_for: I'm looking for a remote-first team where ownership matters, quality is non-negotiable, and I can have real impact on architectural decisions. Small, growing teams that value thinking over ticket velocity.
